config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan bermasyarakat, aturan yang terlalu menekan individu sering dipakai atas nama “kepentingan umum” padahal merugikan kelompok kecil. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan hak pribadi selalu di bawah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society’s interests should automatically take priority over individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how easily that idea can be used to excuse abuse of power. Public safety and shared needs matter, but rights are supposed to set firm limits so the majority cannot just steamroll unpopular people or minorities. In my view, the real test is whether a restriction is narrowly necessary and fair, not whether it can be justified by a vague appeal to the common good. A healthy society protects both, but when there is a conflict, individual rights should not be treated as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu kalau dibuat mutlak, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan aturan yang menekan orang kecil atau minoritas. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, terutama untuk hal seperti keselamatan, kesehatan publik, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh hak individu yang jelas agar negara tidak semena-mena. Justru seperti yang Anda sebut, ukuran utamanya bukan apakah sesuatu menguntungkan mayoritas, melainkan apakah pembatasannya benar-benar perlu, adil, dan tidak berlebihan. Jadi bagi saya, yang benar adalah keseimbangan, bukan prioritas otomatis masyarakat atas individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should get automatic priority over the individual, and I am skeptical of any rule that says it should. In the United States, we have a long history of people invoking the common good to justify censorship, discrimination, surveillance, and other abuses that later looked indefensible. Public health, safety, and order matter, but they only deserve deference when the restriction is necessary, narrowly tailored, and truly fair. If the rights of the individual can be overridden too easily, then “society’s interests” becomes a blank check for the powerful.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu kalau dijadikan prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia frasa “demi kepentingan masyarakat” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan hak orang kecil atau kelompok minoritas. Kepentingan publik memang penting untuk hal seperti keamanan, kesehatan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu tidak boleh jadi alasan otomatis untuk mengorbankan kebebasan individu. Kalau hak individu terlalu gampang diabaikan, justru yang paling diuntungkan adalah pihak yang sudah punya kuasa, bukan masyarakat luas. Bagi saya, ukuran yang benar adalah apakah pembatasan itu benar-benar perlu, adil, dan proporsional, bukan sekadar mengatasnamakan mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that kind of language has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, surveillance, and other abuses that were later defended as “for the common good.” I do think public safety, health, and order can justify limits in specific cases, but only when the limits are necessary, narrow, and fair. If “society’s interests” can override rights too easily, then the powerful get to define the common good in ways that hurt unpopular people and minorities.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
